title: fraxinus bungeana
intro: Fraxinus bungeana, also known as the Manchurian ash tree, is a deciduous tree native to China and Korea. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The bark and leaves of the Fraxinus bungeana contain flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Fraxinus bungeana, such as decoctions and infusions, are used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in modern herbal medicine to support its health benefits.
